chief_complaint: A 58-year-old male patient presented with a persistent high fever during treatment for mild acute pancreatitis.
history: The patient had mild acute pancreatitis and was treated with ulinastatin. He had no evidence of infection, but developed a persistent high fever during treatment.
exam: Vital signs: high fever, no other significant findings.
labs: ECG: normal, Bloods: elevated pancreatic enzymes, Imaging: ultrasound showing mild pancreatitis, no signs of infection.
